anki.deckName: Corebook::Neuro::Brain::Primary Effects of CNS Trauma::Chronic Subdural Hematoma

imageRegistry.caption: Axial graphic depicts pathology of classic chronic subdural hematoma (SDH) with the formation of thin internal , thicker external membranes. Note the stretched bridging veins crossing the SDH, top-to-bottom gradation of fluid from serosanguinous to more proteinaceous contents.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial NECT depicts chronic SDH as a crescentic, hypodense extraaxial fluid collection over the left hemisphere. Stretched cortical veins are faintly visible as they cross the SDH.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial graphic depicts "mixed" SDH. Numerous loculations that contain hemorrhages of multiple ages are present. Note the fluid-fluid levels with mixed acute, subacute blood . Rehemorrhage into preexisting subacute &/or chronic SDH is common.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial NECT shows classic left-sided "mixed" SDH . Note the multiple loculations and fluid-fluid levels formed by acute rehemorrhage into preexisting loculated chronic SDH.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial NECT in an elderly patient with multiple falls shows a "bubbly" fluid collection with loculations and multiple blood-fluid levels .

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial CTA in the same patient shows the "bubbly" crescentic fluid collection. Note the inward displacement of brain with compressed sulci and displaced cortical veins .

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T2* GRE MR in the same patient shows multiple hypointense blooming foci, consistent with hemorrhage into a chronic loculated SDH. Note multiple locules with blood-fluid levels .

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T1 C+ FS MR shows some enhancement around the margins of the chronic SDH , consistent with a membrane of organizing granulation tissue.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial NECT in a patient with Dandy-Walker malformation shows a bilateral chronic SDH with calcified margins . The brain is squeezed toward the midline by the SDH.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: More cephalad NECT in the same case shows margins of the loculated chronic SDHs are heavily calcified . This unusual appearance is sometimes termed "armored brain."

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: This case illustrates the concept of devascularizing membranes of chronic SDHs by embolizing the middle meningeal arteries. This 78-year-old man had recurrent chronic SDHs, shown on this NECT as mixed-density SDHs .

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Lateral DSA of the selective left external carotid artery in the same patient shows the normal middle meningeal artery . The superficial temporal and maxillary arteries are also opacified.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: DSA of the left external carotid artery in the same patient after embolization of the middle meningeal artery (MMA) shows the superficial temporal and maxillary arteries. The MMA has been occluded and is not opacified.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial NECT in the same patient 2 weeks after the left MMA embolization shows the left mixed a/cSDH has become progressively more hypodense. The right cSDH is also slightly more hypodense compared to the initial NECT.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial NECT 6 weeks after the left MMA embolization shows the left cSDH continues to decrease in density and is slightly smaller. The right chronic SDH has almost completely resolved.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial NECT 5 months later shows both chronic SDHs have almost completely resorbed, and there is no evidence for recurrent hemorrhage. One year after MMA embolization, the patient is normal and has had no recurrence of SDHs.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T1 FS MR in a 79-year-old woman with recurrent right SDH is windowed to show the multiple variable-sized locules of mixed-age blood with fluid-fluid levels .

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T2 MR shows multiple loculated pockets within the chronic SDH. There is significant compression of the underlying sulci and gyri. Note the presence of a thick membrane seen here as a linear, slightly irregular hypointensity outside of the loculated collection.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T2* GRE MR shows multiple blooming dependent blood-fluid levels within the chronic SDH . Note the thickened, irregular vascularized membrane also shows gradient susceptibility.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial T1 C+ FS MR shows some enhancement in the sulci underlying the chronic SDH, most likely representing compressed vasculature. Because the mixed chronic SDH contains large amounts of subacute hemorrhage and dilute free methemoglobin, it is difficult to determine if any enhancement is present.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: Axial DWI MR through the cephalad part of the chronic SDH shows restricted diffusion in the vascularized membrane.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageRegistry.caption: ADC confirms restriction in the membrane of the chronic SDH.

visualEvidence.duplicateEvidenceRole: Use this plain/unannotated preview for visual duplicate classification before archiving any image.

imageEvidence.purpose: Visual evidence for duplicate detection. Use these image files before deciding that source images are exact duplicates.
imageEvidence.duplicateClassificationRule: Archive only images proven to be exact/same-slice/same-screenshot duplicates or unusable. Caption-only similarity is not enough; uncertain or merely similar images stay primary as recognition reinforcement.
